summary: Excellent soundboard capture of the Alpine Valley ’89 run, highlighted by a flawless Sugar Magnolia → Scarlet Begonias transition and superb band performance.
review: The recording is crystal‑clear for a 1989 soundboard tape, with only minor mix quirks (a slightly high bass and one reviewer noting odd reverb).  Musically the night shines – the second set rides on an unforgettable Sugar Magnolia into Scarlet Begonias segue, tight jams on Bird Song, Sunshine Daydream and Eyes, and a fiery Touch‑of‑Grey opener that keeps the energy blazing.  Overall the show is celebrated as one of the band’s peak summer performances.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, almost all 4–5 stars, with consistent praise for both performance (standout transitions, tight playing) and recording quality (crisp soundboard).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix; a few comments about bass being a touch high and one note of odd reverb, but generally superb clarity for an ’89 SBD.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows brilliantly, with a high‑energy opener (Touch of Grey → Jack Straw) and a second‑set bookended by Sugar Magnolia and Sunshine Daydream; transitions—especially Sugar→Scarlet—are repeatedly described as seamless.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Consistently praised for his soaring lead work and “blazing” tone throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for aggressive, melodic bass lines (though one reviewer noted the mix had the bass a bit high).
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned only in passing; one critique of a “whiney” Fantasy/Jude segue, otherwise no specific comments.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives glowing accolades as the best keyboardist ever, with standout contributions on Memphis Blues, Mr. Fantasy and throughout the second set.
